signals.reasoning: BTC is still recovering well, but the edge here remains weaker than in the best alt setups and the macro backdrop is still only partially supportive.
ETH looks healthier, but the move still reads like broad market recovery rather than a uniquely actionable fresh signal.
SOL has good rebound structure, but it is already well off the lows and no longer offers a cleaner entry than the strongest squeeze name.
TST is far too extended now. The squeeze is real, but after a move of this size the trade quality is gone unless it resets first.
STABLE has improved, but its current funding support is much weaker than before and the live structure is no longer clean enough to justify a fresh long.
BRETT is constructive, but not in a way that gives a clearer edge than the rest of the board. It is interesting, not compelling.
STBL has recovered strongly, but positive funding and the already-mature move make it a poor fresh entry from here.
WCT still trends well, but it is already extended and the execution quality remains too messy to justify chasing.
MERL looks more like a low-quality rebound than a clean breakout. There is not enough edge here to force a trade.
VVV still reads more like recovery and short-covering than a true fresh trend continuation. It is not broken enough to short and not clean enough to buy.
ZEREBRO remains a high-beta recovery name rather than a durable clean long. The structure is too noisy after the first rebound leg.
